zh.Compton, Berkshire, England, United King: 英國英格蘭波克夏康普頓
zh.Tillières-sur-Avre, Eure, Upper Normandy: 法國豪-諾門迪艾提利艾雷-素爾-阿福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Crispin de Tillières於大約1175年出生在法國豪-諾門迪艾提利艾雷-素爾-阿福時，她的父親Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières56歲，母親Aliénor de   Vitre18歲。她於1198年與Thomas Malmayns在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1237年在英國英格蘭享年63歲。

zh-hans.Compton, Berkshire, England, United King: 英国英格兰波克夏康普頓
zh-hans.Tillières-sur-Avre, Eure, Upper Normandy: 法国豪-諾門迪艾提利艾雷-素爾-阿福
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Crispin de Tillières于大约1175年出生在法国豪-諾門迪艾提利艾雷-素爾-阿福时，她的父亲Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières56岁，母亲Aliénor de   Vitre18岁。她于1198年与Thomas Malmayns在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1237年在英国英格兰享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: Crispin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Pedro, Alicia, Fernando, Manuel, Rafael, Adolfo, Alfredo, Ana, Areli, Candita.  French Adrien, Andre, Eugenie, Francoise.

English; Spanish (Crispín): from the personal name Middle English Crispin, Spanish Crispín, from Latin Crispinus, derived from crispus ‘curly-haired’ (see Crisp ). This name was especially popular in France in the early Middle Ages, having been borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Soissons in AD c. 285 along with a companion, Crispinianus (whose name is a further derivative of the same word). Compare Crespin and 3 below.

English (of Norman origin): from Old French and Middle English crespin, crispin ‘curly-haired’, also found as Crepin; see Crippin . It is difficult to tell this apart from the personal name in 1 above. According to Lanfranc, archbishop of Canterbury (1070–1089), the Norman Gilbert Crispin was the first man to receive this nickname, and two of his sons adopted it as their surname. His grandson Gilbert Crispin became abbot of Westminster in 1085. Members of the Norman family were said to have inherited a shock of hair that stood up stiffly like a bristly pine, as though the name derived from Latin crispus pinus, but this derivation is a medieval invention.

variants.name: Crisp
meanings: English: nickname for a man with curly hair, from Middle English crisp, crips, scrip, Old English crisp, cryps (from Latin crispus), reinforced in Middle English by an Old French word also from Latin crispus. In some cases this is possibly a nickname from the personal name Crispin .
Americanized form of German Krisp: nickname from Middle High German krisp, krispel ‘curly-haired man’.
Americanized form of German Krisp: from a short form of the medieval personal name Krispin (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crippen
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  .
Irish: variant of   Gribbin  .

variants.name: Crespin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Raul, Vidal, Adelina, Alfonso, Amador, Andres, Aniceto, Delfino, Emilio.
Spanish (Crespín): variant of Crispin .
French: from the Latin personal name Crispinus (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crispen
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.
Americanized form of German Krispin, a cognate of Crispin .

variants.name: Crispino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Luigi, Carlo, Amato, Angelo, Antonio, Domenico, Fiore, Gennaro, Michelina, Rocco, Sal, Salvator.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Crispino, from Latin Crispinus, a derivative of the old Roman surname Crispus ‘curly(-headed)’ (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crippin
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  .
Irish: variant of   Gribbin  .

variants.name: Crespi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jaime, Juan, Mercedes, Racquel, Alberto, Alejandro, Consuelo, Eduardo, Jose, Jose Manuel, Migdalia, Pedro.  Jewish Aron, Nissim.
Catalan (Crespí): from a personal name, the Catalan equivalent of Crispin .
Italian (northern): variant of   Crespo  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Crispin de Tillières was born about 1175, in Tillières-sur-Avre, Eure, Upper Normandy, France, her father, Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières, was 56 and her mother, Aliénor de   Vitre, was 18. She married Thomas Malmayns in 1198, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1237, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Crispin de Tillières est née environ 1175, à Tillières-sur-Avre, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France, son père, Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Aliénor de   Vitre, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Malmayns en 1198, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1237, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Crispin de Tillières um 1175 in Tillières-sur-Avre, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières 56 und ihre Mutter Aliénor de   Vitre 18. Sie hat 1198 in England Thomas Malmayns geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1237 im Alter von 63 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Crispin de Tillières è nata il circa 1175 nel Tillières-sur-Avre, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia, sua padre, Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières, aveva 56 anni e sua madre, Aliénor de   Vitre, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Malmayns nel 1198, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1237, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Crispin de Tillièresが約1175年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアティリeレス-スル-アvアで生まれた時，父Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillièresは56歳，母Aliénor de   Vitreは18歳であった。1198年にイギリスでThomas Malmaynsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1237年にイギリスイギリスで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Crispin de Tillières(이)가 대략 1175년 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르 틸리e레스-수-아브르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières(은)는 56세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Aliénor de   Vitre(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1198년 영국에서 Thomas Malmayns(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1237년 영국 잉글랜드에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Crispin de Tillières nasceu aproximadamente 1175, em Tillières-sur-Avre, Eure, Haute-Normandie, França, seu pai, Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières, tinha 56 anos e sua mãe, Aliénor de   Vitre, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Malmayns em 1198, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1237, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Crispin de Tillières родилась около 1175 г. в местности тиллиeрес-сур-авр, эур, хауте-норманди, Франция, её отцу по имени Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Aliénor de   Vitre, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Malmayns в 1198 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1237 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Crispin de Tillières nació aproximadamente 1175, en Tillières-sur-Avre, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières, tenía 56 años y su mamá, Aliénor de   Vitre, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Thomas Malmayns en 1198, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1237, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 63 años.
